title: Tanacetum serotinum
intro: Tanacetum serotinum, also known as late flowering tansy or late tansy, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family that is native to Europe and western Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and menstrual cramps, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum serotinum include sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone and dehydrocostus lactone, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Tanacetum serotinum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health complaints, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
taxonomy: Tanacetum serotinum is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Tanacetum and is a perennial herb. The species is native to Europe and has a range of distribution across the continent. Tanacetum serotinum is a relatively rare and lesser-known member of the tanacetum genus. Further research on its classification may provide more specific and detailed information.
uses: Tanacetum serotinum, also known as autumn pyrethrum, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its flowers are said to have a calming effect and have been used to treat anxiety and insomnia. The plant is also believed to have antiviral and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for wound healing. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Tanacetum serotinum for medicinal purposes. It is often used in herbal teas and tinctures, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.

parts: Tanacetum serotinum is also known as the late flowering tansy. The medicinal parts of Tanacetum serotinum primarily include the leaves, flowers, and roots. The leaves and flowers are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and inflammation. preparations: Tanacetum serotinum, also known as autumnal tansy, is sometimes used in herbal preparations for its potential medicinal properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety. Some herbalists recommend using the plant to support digestive health and to aid in the relief of menstrual cramps. side_effects: Tanacetum serotinum, also known as autumn chamomile, is generally considered safe when used in moderation. However, consuming large quantities may cause stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. It's also essential to note that pregnant and breastfeeding women should exercise caution when using this plant due to its potential effects on fetal development and infant health. In rare cases, autumn chamomile may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and sedatives, which can lead to adverse effects. As with any herbal remedy, it's recommended to consult a healthcare professional before using Tanacetum serotinum for medicinal purposes.
